info.subtitle: Information
info.app_desc: SolarOptimizer3D is a tool for optimizing the placement of photovoltaic panels on 3D buildings. It allows modeling parametric or imported buildings, simulating solar paths and shadows via ray-casting, and optimizing panel layouts using the Seed-and-Grow algorithm.
info.algo_sag_desc: Spatial greedy heuristic algorithm: identifies the point of maximum irradiance and expands the layout outward using Von Neumann neighbors, automatically selecting the optimal orientation.
info.algo_ray_desc: Grid of sample points on the roof, with ray-casting toward the solar position for every hour of the year. Includes seasonal transmissivity for trees.
info.algo_noct_desc: Power de-rating based on cell temperature vs STC conditions (25°C), using thermal coefficient and NOCT temperature from the panel catalog.
info.contacts_desc: For reports, suggestions or collaborations you can open an
info.contacts_suffix: or contact the author by email.

tooltips.building_width: Building width in meters (5–30m). Default: 12m, typical for residential buildings.
tooltips.building_height: Eaves height in meters (3–15m). Default: 6m, corresponding to a 2-story building.
tooltips.roof_type: Roof type. Flat: horizontal surface. Gable: two symmetric sloped surfaces with a ridge. Hip: four sloped surfaces converging toward a reduced ridge.
tooltips.roof_angle: Roof slope angle (0–60°). Default: 30°, optimal value for European latitudes. Affects usable area and received irradiance.
tooltips.ridge_height: Ridge height above eaves (1–10m). Determines the slope of hip roof surfaces.
tooltips.ridge_length: Hip roof ridge length (0 – width-1 m). Lower values create a more pyramidal roof.
tooltips.rotation: Building orientation relative to North (0°=N, 90°=E, 180°=S, 270°=W). Affects solar irradiance and shadows.
tooltips.ground_height: Vertical offset of the imported model from ground level (-10 to +10m). Useful for aligning the building base to the ground plane.
tooltips.latitude: Site latitude in decimal degrees. Default: 41.9°N (Rome). Determines sun position in the sky and received irradiance.
tooltips.longitude: Site longitude in decimal degrees. Default: 12.5°E (Rome). Affects local solar time.
tooltips.analysis_mode: Annual: weighted average over the entire year. Monthly: analysis for a single month. Instant: shadow at a specific moment (date + time).
tooltips.shadow_resolution: Sample points per side of the shadow grid. Medium (30 pt): fast, suitable for preliminary assessments. High (50 pt): more accurate, recommended for final design.
tooltips.max_peak_power: Maximum installable peak power in kWp (1–50). The algorithm stops when this limit is reached. Default: 6 kWp, typical for residential systems.
tooltips.system_efficiency: Overall system efficiency (50–100%). Accounts for wiring, inverter, soiling and mismatch losses. Default: 86% (PVGIS).
tooltips.obstacle_type: Obstacle type. Chimney, dormer, antenna: placed on the roof. Tree, building: placed on the ground. Each type has specific default geometry and dimensions.
tooltips.obstacle_dims: Obstacle dimensions in meters. Width, height and depth of the bounding box.
tooltips.trunk_height: Tree trunk height in meters. The canopy starts above the trunk.
tooltips.canopy_radius: Tree canopy radius in meters. Determines the shading area and geometry size.
tooltips.tree_shape: Canopy shape: cone (typical conifer), sphere (broadleaf/oak), umbrella (maritime pine), columnar (cypress).
tooltips.foliage_type: Deciduous: loses leaves in winter (high transmissivity Nov–Mar, reduced shadows). Evergreen: provides constant shading year-round.
tooltips.rotation_y: Obstacle rotation around the vertical axis (0–355°). For symmetric objects (cylinders, antennas) the effect is purely visual.
tooltips.stringing_field: Required for string sizing. Without this parameter, automatic calculation of panels per string will not be available.
tooltips.installed_power: Total peak power of the system in kWp, calculated as N° panels × unit power of the selected module. Represents the maximum generation capacity under STC conditions (1000 W/m², 25°C).
tooltips.panel_count: Total number of PV modules placed by the optimization algorithm or manually in the installation zone.
tooltips.annual_production: Estimated annual energy production (kWh/year), calculated by summing monthly production accounting for: POA irradiance, shading, thermal losses (NOCT model), module degradation and system losses (BOS).
tooltips.specific_yield: Ratio between annual energy produced (kWh) and installed power (kWp). Typical values for Italy: 1100–1500 kWh/kWp. Higher values indicate better irradiance and less shading.
tooltips.monthly_production_chart: Bar chart of estimated monthly production (kWh/month). Summer shows higher values due to greater irradiance and longer days. The gradient-colored bars reflect the relative irradiance of each month.
tooltips.monthly_irradiance_chart: Monthly irradiance on the Plane of Array (POA) in kWh/m². Represents the solar energy effectively reaching the panel surface, accounting for tilt and orientation.
tooltips.daily_simulation_chart: Hourly comparison between ideal power (clear-sky, no shading) and actual power (with shading and thermal losses) for the selected day. The area between the two curves represents shading and temperature losses.
tooltips.self_consumption_rate: Percentage of produced energy consumed directly on-site, without being fed into the grid. Depends on consumption profile and simultaneity between production and load.
tooltips.grid_feed_in: Excess energy fed into the grid, remunerated at the feed-in tariff (GSE). Higher self-consumption means lower grid feed-in.
tooltips.annual_savings: Annual savings on the electricity bill, calculated as: self-consumed energy × energy price. Does not include feed-in revenue.
tooltips.payback_period: Investment payback period in years, calculated as: system cost / (annual bill savings + annual feed-in revenue). Does not account for tax incentives, inflation or tariff changes.
tooltips.annual_surface: 3D surface showing the system power for every hour of every day of the year. Height represents power in kW, color represents POA solar irradiance (W/m²). Rotate and zoom with the mouse to explore.
tooltips.consumption_analysis: Detailed statistical analysis of the uploaded hourly consumption profile. Indicators and charts show consumption patterns across different time scales, useful for proper system sizing and maximizing self-consumption.
